info.title: MDM API V1
info.description: Workspace ONE UEM MDM REST API V1

post.summary: New - Executes the admin action for the set of devices after performing necessary checks like command accessibility, device enrollment status, support for command on device etc.
post.description: Execute Admin Action for specified set of devices

parameters.name: adminAction
parameters.description: The command to execute [ENTERPRISE_WIPE, DEVICE_WIPE, REBOOT, SUSPEND, WAKEUP,SYNC_DEVICE,SHUTDOWN, DEVICE_INFORMATION,SEND_MESSAGE,REQUEST_DEVICE_LOG].(Required).

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: Model containing the command XML passed to the API.(Required).

400.description: 1. {5102: Command is not supported.}  2. {5138: Feature is currently turned off}  3. {10036: Admin action cannot be performed on more than {0} number of devices in one go.}

get.summary: New - Gets the list of Remote View destinations configured in the organization group.
get.description: This api is used to get list of destinations for the organization group. Destination details like mac address, ip address, pk, pi  are required for remote viewing the iOS device.

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: Id of organization group.

404.description: 1. {400: "Resource's organization group not found or user does not have access to this organization group."}

put.summary: New - Update the destination details of a previously configured Remote View destination.
put.description: iOS device can be remotely viewed by using software like AirServer or Reflector, it is required to save the details of the destination device like mac address, ip address, pk, pi. This endpoint is used to edit the details of a previously configured destination for the organization group.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: RemoteViewDestination data.

404.description: 1. {5069: "Destination device not found for the specified id."}2. {400: "Resource's organization group not found or user does not have access to this organization group."}

post.summary: New - Add a destination for Remote View.
post.description: iOS device can be remotely viewed by using software like AirServer or Reflector. It is required to save the details of the destination device like mac address, ip address, pk, pi. This endpoint is used to save the destination details for the organization group.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: RemoteViewDestination data.

400.description: 1. {400: "Resource's organization group not found or user does not have access to this organization group."}

422.description: 1012 - Unprocessable Entity 1. {Element: RemoteViewDestinationV1Model.destinationMacAddress Message: Invalid Destination Mac Address value.}2. {Element: RemoteViewDestinationV1Model.destinationIPAddress Message: Invalid Destination Ip Address value.}3. {Element: RemoteViewDestinationV1Model.model Message: Invalid Destination Model value.}4. {Element: RemoteViewDestinationV1Model.srcvers Message: Invalid Destination Srcver value.}5. {Element: RemoteViewDestinationV1Model.features Message: Invalid Destination Features value.}6. {Element: RemoteViewDestinationV1Model.pk Message: Invalid Destination Pk value.}7. {Element: RemoteViewDestinationV1Model.pi Message: Invalid Destination Pi value.}8. {Element: RemoteViewDestinationV1Model.vv Message: Invalid Destination VV value.}

get.summary: New - Gets Remote View destination details for the device.
get.description: This api is used to get the details of the specified destination. Destination details like mac address, ip address, pk, pi  are required for remote viewing the iOS device.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Id of the Remote View destination device.

404.description: 1. {5069: "Destination device not found for the specified id."}

delete.summary: New - Delete a Remote View destination for the device.
delete.description: This api is used to delete the destination details for the organization group, destination details like mac address, ip address, pk, pi  are required for remote viewing the iOS device.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Id of Remote View destination device to be deleted.

404.description: 1. {5069: "Destination device not found for the specified id."}

get.summary: Retrieves application details of the device identified by device ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The device ID (Required).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The specific page number to get.

parameters.name: pagesize
parameters.description: Max records per page.

400.description: Bad Request 1.{400- Device does not exist or Admin User does not have access to the Device.}

404.description: Not Found 1.{404- Device not found for this request.}

501.description: Not Implemented 1.{501- Functionality not supported for device type : {0}}

get.summary: Retrieves application details of the device identified by alternate id.
get.description:
Fetches all application details for the device identified by alternate ID other than device ID.
Alternate device identifiers can be MAC address, IMEI, Serial number or UDID.
Example : IMEI-BCG84753GH, Serial Number-573489754.

parameters.name: searchBy
parameters.description: The alternate id type [Macaddress, Udid, Serialnumber, ImeiNumber].

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Device alternate id.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The specific page number to get.

parameters.name: pagesize
parameters.description: Max records per page.

404.description: Not Found 1.{5002- Device not found}

422.description: Unprocessable Entity 1.{5003- Invalid SearchBy Parameter, 5208- Invalid Id Parameter}

get.summary: Retrieves Admin applications details for passed DeviceID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The device ID.

get.summary: New - Returns the apps which are applicable to the device.
get.description: This endpoint returns the details of apps which are assigned or installed on the device matching the search text. Following special characters are not allowed in query params ('[', ']', '(', ')', '{', '}', '&lt;', '&gt;', '\"').

parameters.name: deviceUuid
parameters.description:
The uuid of the device.
            Accepted formats **guid**
            E.g. 3d958f38-246e-4854-a306-189d941ab073(Required).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number which will be fetched, 0 based index. Default 0.

parameters.name: pagesize
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to be returned in one page. Default 500.

parameters.name: searchtext
parameters.description: If provided, the records matching this text will be returned. The search will be applied on the following properties [name, installed_version, assigned_version]. The default value will be empty string.

parameters.name: sortorder
parameters.description: Whether the sort order is ascending or descending. The property used for sorting is name. Possible values [Asc, Desc]. Default value is Asc.

403.description: 1. {5143: User does not have access to the device.}

404.description: 1. {5061: Device not found for this request.}

get.summary: New - Returns details for the specified app installed/assigned to the device.
get.description: This endpoint returns the details of specified app which is assigned or installed on the device.

parameters.name: deviceUuid
parameters.description:
The uuid of the device.
            Accepted formats **guid**
            E.g. 3d958f38-246e-4854-a306-189d941ab073(Required).

parameters.name: bundleid
parameters.description:
The bundle id of the application.
            E.g. com.tencent.ig(Required)

400.description: 1. {5005: Application Bundle Id Must Be Specified.}

403.description: 1. {5143: User does not have access to the device.}

404.description: 1. {5061: Device not found for specified UUID.} 2. {5005: The application is not assigned or installed on the device.}

post.summary: New - Save asset metadata and payloads.
post.description: Save asset general information, assignment and payload details as a metadata JSON string.

parameters.name: organizationGroupUuid
parameters.description: Unique identifier for organization group.(Required).

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: (Required).

parameters.name: assetSaveRequest
parameters.description: Asset entity save request model.(Required).

200.description: Returns saved asset response model which holds asset uuid and asset name.

400.description: 1. { 7022: Invalid asset parameters. } 2. { 7023: Failed to save asset. } 3. { 7019: The Input organization-group-uuid: {0} is Invalid }

403.description: 1. { 7020: The user does not have access to input organization-group-uuid: {0}. }

404.description: 1. { 6183: The Input organization-group-uuid: {0} is not found. }

get.summary: New - Gets the asset metadata for the specified asset uuid.
get.description: Gets the asset general information and payload details as a metadata JSON string.

parameters.name: organizationGroupUuid
parameters.description: Unique identifier for organization group.(Required).

parameters.name: assetUuid
parameters.description: Unique identifier for asset.(Required).

parameters.name: culture
parameters.description: Specifies the language culture of metadata.

parameters.name: include-linked-resources
parameters.description: Specifies whether linked resources should be included in the response.

400.description: 1. { 6: Query parameter is invalid. } 2. { 20: General Server Error. } 3. { 7010: Metadata transform service return errors. } 4. { 7019: The Input organization-group-uuid: {0} is Invalid }

403.description: 1. { 7020: The user does not have access to input organization-group-uuid: {0}. }

404.description: 1. { 6183: The input organization group, {0} was not found. } 2. { 7024: Invalid asset unique identifier {0}. }

get.summary: New - Gets the list of latest version of assets by applying the search criteria.
get.description: Gets the list of latest version of assets by applying the search criteria.

parameters.name: organization-group-uuid
parameters.description: Unique identifier for organization group.(Required)

parameters.name: payload-key

parameters.name: term

400.description: 1. { 7019: The Input organization-group-uuid: {0} is Invalid }

403.description: 1. { 7020: The user does not have access to input organization-group-uuid: {0}. }

post.summary: New - Get a summary of assignment preview devices for the given asset.
post.description: Get a summary of assigned preview devices for the given asset.

parameters.name: request
parameters.description: Request model to get assignment preview summary.(Required).

400.description: Bad Request  1. {6183: Resource's organization group: {organization_group_uuid} not found or user does not have access to this organization group. }

post.summary: Searches for devices assigned to the specified profiles.
post.description: Devices assigned to the specified profiles as last evaluated during device check-in.

parameters.name: request
parameters.description: Request model to find devices assigned to the asset.(Required).

400.description: Bad Request  1. {6183: Resource's organization group: {organization_group_uuid} not found or user does not have access to this organization group. }

422.description: Unprocessable Entity 1. {1012: Included smart group uuid's must be supplied.} 2. {1012: Applicability filter must be supplied.}

get.summary: New - Returns a list of Assignment Groups matching the search criteria
get.description:
Get List of Assignment Groups based on the Organization Group, Search Text, AssignmentType.
Search text should not be more than 255 characters.

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: Unique Identifier for the Organization Group(Required)

parameters.name: assignmentType
parameters.description:
Type of Assignment Group to search
             0 - All types of Assignment Groups (Smart Group, User Group, Organization Group) 
             1 - Smart Group 
             2 - User Group 
             3 - Organization Group(Required)

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name of the Assignment Group by which result will be filtered (Optional). When not passed, all Assignment Groups present in that organization will be returned.

parameters.name: deviceType
parameters.description: device type (Optional). When not passed, all device type count will be returned.

400.description: 1.{5005: Invalid Assignment Group Type. Should be 0-All 1-SmartGroup 2-UserGroup 3-OrganizationGroup}, 2.{Custom Attribute Search Text should be a maximum of 255 characters.}, 3.{400: Resource's organization group not found or user does not have access to this organization group.}

404.description: {5060: No Assignment Groups were found}

get.summary: New - Returns a list of Organizational Units matching the search criteria.
get.description: Gets a list of Organizational Units based on the passed  organization group identifier and optional OU name filter search text. Search text should not be more than 255 characters.

parameters.name: organizationGroupUuid
parameters.description: Unique Identifier for the Organization Group(Required).

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name of the Organizational Unit by which result will be filtered (Optional). When not passed, all Organizational Units present in that organization will be returned.

404.description: {5061: No Organizational Units were found}

get.summary: Retrieves certificate details of the device identified by device Id.
get.description: Certificate details contains information like the sampling period, error details, name, expiration, issued date and the status.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Device identifier (Example: 3010055).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Specific page number to get. 0 based index.

parameters.name: pagesize
parameters.description: Maximumm records per page. Default 500.

get.summary: Retrieves certificate details of the device identified by the alternate Id's.
get.description: Certficate details contains information like the sampling period, error details, name, expiration, issued date and the status. This API allows searching a device using alternate Id's specified in the searchby parameter.

parameters.name: searchby
parameters.description: The alternate id type; possible values: [Macaddress, Udid, Serialnumber, ImeiNumber] (Required).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Device alternate id (Example: Macaddress: 848506B900BA, Udid: 6bf0f04c73681fbecfc3eb4f13cbf05b, SerialNumber: LGH871c18f631a, ImeiNumber: 354833052322837) (Required).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Specific page number to get. 0 based index.

parameters.name: pagesize
parameters.description: Maximumm records per page. Default 500.

404.description: {5002: Device not found}

422.description: {5003: Invalid SearchBy Parameter}

post.summary: New - Create a new Device Policy profile for the given organization Group
post.description: Device Policy profile will have customized settings that can be applied on a Chrome device. These settings include auto-Launched Kiosk App,device Heartbeat monitor rate,application Settings for specifying list of apps that can be force-installed on a device,Email/SMS deviceStatus Alert Delivery settings etc.

parameters.name: formData
parameters.description: Kiosk Device policy to manage a chrome device(Required)

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: Organization group Identifier where the devicePolicy profile needs to be created.(Required)

400.description: 1. {5160: At least one payload must be configured in order to save a profile} 2. {5012: Resource's organization group: {0} not found or user does not have access to this organization group.}

get.summary: New - Retrieve the existing Policy profile
get.description: Device/User Policy profile will have customized settings (key/value pairs with pre-defined airwatch specific keys) that can be applied on a Chrome device.These settings include auto-Launched Kiosk App,device Heartbeat monitor rate,application Settings for specifying list of apps that can be force-installed on a device,Email/SMS deviceStatus Alert Delivery settings etc. *On editing a cloud policy, new version of the settings will be created.*

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: The identifier for the organization group where the policy is being requested.(Required)

parameters.name: policyId
parameters.description: AW internal devicePolicy profile identifier(Required)

400.description: 1. {5012: Resource's organization group: {0} not found or user does not have access to this organization group.}

put.summary: New - Edit the existing Device Policy profile by creating a version of the settings
put.description: Device Policy profile will have customized settings (key/value pairs with pre-defined airwatch specific keys) that can be applied on a Chrome device.These settings include auto-Launched Kiosk App,device Heartbeat monitor rate,applicationSettings for specifying list of apps that can be force-installed on a device,Email/SMS deviceStatus Alert Delivery settings etc. *On editing a cloud policy, new version of the settings will be created.*

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: Organization group Identifier where the devicePolicy profile needs to be updated.(Required)

parameters.name: policyId
parameters.description: AW internal devicePolicy profile identifier(Required)

parameters.name: devicePolicyData
parameters.description: Kiosk Device policy to manage a chrome device(Required)

400.description: Bad request

get.summary: New - Get Metadata for loading device Policy cloud profile UI
get.description: Device or User Policy profile will have customized settings (key/value pairs with pre-defined airwatch specific keys) that can be applied on a Chrome device.These settings include network, VPN, URL Access control, Sign In, auto-Launched Kiosk App,device Heartbeat monitor rate,application Settings for specifying list of apps that can be force-installed on a device,Email/SMS deviceStatus Alert Delivery settings etc. *On editing a cloud profile, new version of the settings will be created.*

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: The identifier for the organization group where the metadata is being requested.(Required)

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: AW internal devicePolicy profile identifier(Required)

parameters.name: profileType
parameters.description: Type of profile 1 is Device Profile 2 is User Profile(Required)

parameters.name: readOnly
parameters.description: Boolean flag to determine if the metadata returned should render the form in read only mode.

400.description: 1. {5012: Resource's organization group: {0} not found or user does not have access to this organization group.}

post.summary: New - Executes device log request command for device matching the filter criteria.
post.description: This api will execute deviceLog command on the device matching the filter criteria. The logs will become available to an admin on AirWatch Console.

parameters.name: logParams
parameters.description: Contains parameters for device log request.(Required).

parameters.name: searchby
parameters.description: Search by alternate id type. Possible values = [Macaddress, Udid, Serialnumber, ImeiNumber] {Formats= Macaddress - 848506B900BA, Udid - 6bf0f04c73681fbecfc3eb4f13cbf05b, SerialNumber - LGH871c18f631a, ImeiNumber - 354833052322837}(Required)

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Device id(Required)

400.description: 1. {6213: Device is not enrolled.} 2. {5245: Invalid DeviceLogLevel for macOS. Supported values are Debug, Information.} 3. {5267: Request device network log command requires Android OS 8+.} 4. {5268: Request device network log command requires Android Hub 20.05+.} 5. {5269: Request device network log command requires Device Owner Enrollment Mode.} 6. {5270: Request device network log command requires Corporate Dedicated ownership.} 7. {5271: Request device network log command requires Collect and Display for IP Address privacy setting.} 8. {5277: Request device security log command requires Android OS 7+.} 9. {5278: Request device security log command requires Device Owner or AOSP Enrollment Mode.} 10. {5279: Request device security log command requires Android Hub version 21.06+.} 11. {5061: Device not found for specified id} 12. {1021: DeviceLogDuration must be between 1 to 720} 13. {21: Functionality not supported for device type {0}.} 14. {1600009: System logging is not supported on COPE devices running Android 11 and above.} 15. {200040: Device log is not supported for the given device.} 16. {5319: Invalid LogSource for iOS. Supported value is Agent.} 17. {5320: Invalid DeviceLogLevel for iOS.}

404.description: 1. {5061: Device not found for specified id}

412.description: 1. {5146: Previous device log request is still In Progress}

422.description: 1. {1012: Invalid searchby parameter}

post.summary: New - Executes stop device log request command for device.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Device id(Required)

400.description: 1. {400: Device is not enrolled.}

404.description: 1. {5061: Device not found for specified id}

422.description: 1. {1012: Invalid searchby parameter}

post.summary: New - Executes Schedule OS Update command for devices in bulk.
post.description: This API will execute schedule OS Update command for multiple devices. For iOS 10.3 and later, devices must be supervised. Prior to iOS 10.3, devices must be supervised and DEP enrolled. (iOS only)

parameters.name: bulkInput
parameters.description: List of device identifiers of the type specified in the searchby parameter.(Required).

parameters.name: searchby
parameters.description: Search by alternate id type. Possible values = [Macaddress, Udid, Serialnumber, ImeiNumber] {Formats= Macaddress - 848506B900BA, Udid - 6bf0f04c73681fbecfc3eb4f13cbf05b, SerialNumber - LGH871c18f631a, ImeiNumber - 354833052322837}(Required)

parameters.name: installaction
parameters.description: Install action type for OS update. Possible Values = [Default, DownloadOnly, InstallASAP](Required)

post.summary: New - Schedule OS Update for supervised DEP devices
post.description: Executes the Schedule OS Update command on the specified device. For iOS 10.3 and later, devices must be supervised. Prior to iOS 10.3, devices must be supervised and DEP enrolled. For macOS, the device must be DEP enrolled.

parameters.description: List of macOS Update product keys. This field is required and only for macOS devices. Ex. ["macOSUpdate15D100"](Required).

parameters.name: searchby
parameters.description: Search by alternate id type. Possible values = [Macaddress, Udid, Serialnumber, ImeiNumber, Uuid] {Formats= Macaddress - 848506B900BA, Udid - 6bf0f04c73681fbecfc3eb4f13cbf05b, SerialNumber - LGH871c18f631a, ImeiNumber - 354833052322837, Uuid - EFA9E128-DE1B-404A-A3F4-E5824A666681}(Required)

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Device alternate ID(Required)

parameters.name: osupdateproductversion
parameters.description: A single iOS Update product version. This field is required and only for iOS devices. Ex. 12.3.1

parameters.name: installaction
parameters.description: Install action type for OS update. Possible Values = [Default, DownloadOnly, InstallASAP, NotifyOnly, InstallLater](Required)

400.description: 1. {400: Device is not enrolled} 2. {5099: Install Action is not supported for iOS devices.}       3. {5098: Invalid Install Action for schedule OS update command}  4. {1043: Missing OS Update product key or OS Update product version}   5. {1044: Only OS Update product key or OS Update product version must be specified but not both}  6. {1045: Unsupported OS Update product version is specified for the targeted device} 7. {1047: OS Update product version must be specified for iOS Devices} 8. {6199: The device must be a DEP device.}

404.description: 1. {5061: Device not found for specified id}

post.summary: New - Executes commands for the specified device.
post.description: Executes the command for the device after performing necessary checks like command accessibility, device enrollment status, support for command on device etc. Supported commands are Lock, EnterpriseWipe, DeviceWipe, DeviceQuery, ClearPasscode, SyncDevice, StopAirPlay, ScheduleOsUpdate, CustomMdmCommand (iOS, macOS, and tvOS only), InstallPackagedMacOSXAgent (macOS only), SoftReset, Shutdown, EnterpriseReset, SyncSensors (macOS only), OsUpdateStatus (iOS and macOS only), RotateFileVaultKey (macOS only), RotateDEPAdminPassword (macOS only), ResetBiosPassword (WinRT only), UserList (iOS only), SyncWorkflows (macOS and WinRT only), InstallSeededScepProfile (Windows only), PauseWindowsUpdates/PauseQualityUpdates/PauseRollbackUpdates (WindowsOnly).

parameters.name: deviceid
parameters.description: Id of the device on which command is to be executed.(Required).

parameters.name: customCommandModel
parameters.description: Applicable only for Apple devices. Required if the command type is CustomMdmCommand. Model containing the custom XML for the command.

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: The command to execute [Lock, EnterpriseWipe, DeviceWipe, DeviceQuery, ClearPasscode, SyncDevice, StopAirPlay, ScheduleOsUpdate, CustomMdmCommand, InstallPackagedMacOSXAgent, SoftReset, Shutdown, EnterpriseReset, SyncSensors, OsUpdateStatus, RotateFileVaultKey, RotateDEPAdminPassword, ResetBiosPassword, UserList, SyncWorkflows, InstallSeededScepProfile].(Required)

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: Applicable only for Chrome Os Devices. Enterprise Wipe reason must be one of the following values - different_model_replacement, retiring_device, same_model_replacement.

parameters.name: keep_apps_on_device
parameters.description: Obsolete, now has no effect. Previously applicable for Windows devices and Enterprise Wipe command, was used to keep or remove apps on the device upon Enterprise Wipe.

parameters.name: update_type
parameters.description: Applicable only for Windows devices and Updates command. Update must be one of the following values - QualityUpdates, FeatureUpdates.

400.description: 1. {400: Command XML cannot be null or empty.} 2. {400: The device type is invalid.} 3. {21: This feature is only supported by macOS devices.} 4. {5060: This feature is not supported for the operating system version on the device.} 5. {400: Device is not enrolled.} 6. {400: Privacy Settings does not allow Device Command: {Command} for the Device Id: {DeviceId}.} 7. {8: Device not found for this request.} 8. {6: Device does not exist or Admin User does not have access to the Device.} 9. {21: Functionality not supported for device type : {DeviceType}.} 10. {6: ScheduleOsUpdate command cannot be sent to Device.} 11. {6: Functionality not supported for device type : {DeviceType}.} 12. {5093: Admin is not authorized to rotate the recovery key}             13. {6: The device type is invalid.} 14. {5100: Device is not enrolled via Automated Device Enrollment.} 15. {5102: Random admin password is not enabled for Device Enrollment Profile used. This password will not be rotated.} 16. {400: Enterprise wipe Reason cannot be null or empty.} 17. {6: Chrome Os Enterprise Wipe reason must be one of the following : different_model_replacement, retiring_device, same_model_replacement.} 18. {6: Device should be encrypted before rotating its recovery key} 19. {5102: Command is not supported.} 20. {5234: Device Hub Version present on device is not supported for this feature.} 21. {30020: DEP Admin account identifier is missing, password can't be rotated. Queued device information command for the deviceId {DeviceId}.}

403.description: 1. {400: Feature is currently turned off.} 2. {5093: User is not authorized to rotate admin password.} 3. {1011: User doesn't have sufficient permissions to perform this operation.}

404.description: 1. {404: Device not found for specified id} 2. {400: Device not found for this request.}

422.description: 1.{422: Invalid command type} 2.{422: WIPE_PROTECTED is supported only on Windows 10 version 1703 and above.} 3.{422: WIPE_PERSIST_USER_DATA is supported only on Windows 10 version 1706 and above.}

post.summary: New - Executes a command for device by alternate ID.

Supported commands are Lock, EnterpriseWipe, DeviceWipe, DeviceQuery, ClearPasscode, SyncDevice, StopAirPlay, ScheduleOsUpdate, CustomMdmCommand (iOS, macOS, and tvOS only), InstallPackagedMacOSXAgent (macOS only), SoftReset, Shutdown, EnterpriseReset, SyncSensors (macOS only), OsUpdateStatus (iOS and macOS only), RotateFileVaultKey (macOS only), RotateDEPAdminPassword (macOS only), InstallSeededScepProfile (Windows Only), ResetBiosPassword (WinRT only).

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: Applicable only for Chrome Os Devices. Enterprise Wipe reason must be one of the following values - different_model_replacement, retiring_device, same_model_replacement.

400.description: 1. {400: Command XML cannot be null or empty} 2. {5100: The device is not eligible for {command} 3. {400: Enterprise wipe Reason cannot be null or empty.} 4. {6: Chrome Os Enterprise Wipe reason must be one of the following : different_model_replacement, retiring_device, same_model_replacement.} 5. {6: Device should be encrypted before rotating its recovery key} 6. {5102: Command is not supported.} 7. {21: Functionality not supported for device type : {DeviceType}.} 8. {5234: Device Hub Version present on device is not supported for this feature.} 9. {30020: DEP Admin account identifier is missing, password can't be rotated. Device custom attributes can be updated using device uuid or serial number. Optionally user name can be used with serial number to uniquely identify the device.

422.description: Validation Failed 1. {6: 'name' and 'value' are required for each custom attribute.} 2. {6: 'applicationGroup' must not be empty.}

parameters.description: Organization group id to be searched, user's OG is considered if not sent.

parameters.name: deviceid
parameters.description: Device id to search.

parameters.name: startdate
parameters.description: Filters the custom attributes which are modified after this datetime.

parameters.name: enddate
parameters.description: Filters the custom attributes which are modified before this datetime.

parameters.name: applicationgroup
parameters.description: Application group to search.

parameters.name: customattributename
parameters.description: Custom attribute name to search.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Custom attribute source to search.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Specific page number to get. 0 based index.

parameters.name: pagesize
parameters.description: Maximum records per page. Default 500.

get.summary: Returns a collection of Automated Device Enrollment profiles based on the search criteria.
get.description: Returns a collection of Automated Device Enrollment profiles based on the search criteria specified. The search parameters can be organization group ID, page, and the pagesize.

parameters.name: OrganizationGroupUuid
parameters.description: Organization Group UUID. (Example:FFD1521E-70D7-4673-A0EF-62938079C0E8, FFD1521E-70D7-4673-A0EF-62938079C0E8)

parameters.name: SearchText
parameters.description: Profile name.

parameters.name: Page
parameters.description: Specific page number to get. 0 based index

parameters.name: PageSize
parameters.description: Maximum records per page. Default 50

parameters.name: OrderBy
parameters.description: Order By. Default Asc (Example:Asc,Dsc)

parameters.name: SortOrder
parameters.description: Sort Order. Default DeviceProfileName (Example:DeviceProfileName,RootLocationGroupName)

400.description: 1. {5056: The organization group format is incorrect. Please enter a valid UUID.}

post.summary: New - Create a new DEP account for the organization group.
post.description: Creates a new Device Enrollment Program account for the given organization group ID based on the provided token and certificate. This account will be used to make all subsequent requests to the Apple server.

parameters.description: This is the organization group identifier also known as GroupID. Typically used during enrollment.(Required).

parameters.description: The dep Account Creation Model containing the token ID and the certificate ID that will be used to generate the device enrollment program account for the organization group. (Required).

get.summary: New - Get Device details by Alternate id.

Get the device details by an alternate id which can be any one of the following ids:
1. Macaddress, 2. Udid, 3. Serialnumber, 4. ImeiNumber, 5. EasId, 6. DeviceId.

parameters.name: searchby
parameters.description: The alternate id type; possible values: [Macaddress, Udid, Serialnumber, ImeiNumber, EasId, DeviceId]. (Formats: Macaddress: 848506B900BA, Udid: 6bf0f04c73681fbecfc3eb4f13cbf05b, SerialNumber: LGH871c18f631a, ImeiNumber: 354833052322837, EasId: 1234, DeviceId: 1234).(Required)

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Device alternate id.(Required)

404.description: 1. {Device not found for specified identifier} 2. {5002: Device not found}

get.summary: New - Searches devices and its custom attributes.
get.description: Searches devices and its custom attributes using the query information provided. A list of custom attributes to search can be given as a query parameter. The search results are limited to the requested custom attributes.

parameters.name: organizationgroupid
parameters.description: OrganizationGroup to be searched. Admin's OG is considered if this is not sent.

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: Device Platform [Android, Apple, AppleOsX, AppleTv, Athena, AveryDennisonPrinter, ChromeBook, ChromeOS, Epson, Motorola, Qnx, Symbian, ToshibaPrinter, WindowsMobile, WindowsPc, WindowsPhone8, WinRT, ZebraPrinter].

parameters.name: seensince
parameters.description: Filters devices such that devices with last seen after this date will be returned.

parameters.name: seentill
parameters.description: Filters devices such that devices with last seen till this date will be returned.

parameters.name: deviceid
parameters.description: Device Identifier.

parameters.name: customattributes
parameters.description: Custom attribute names.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Specific page number to get. 0 based index.

parameters.name: pagesize
parameters.description: Maximum records per page. Default 500.

400.description: Bad Request   1. {Bad Request - Invalid date value {} for seensince/seentill. Valid DateTime formats are : yyyy/MM/dd, yyyy-MM-dd, MM/dd/yyyy, MM-dd-yyyy, yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.fff, yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.fff, MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss.fff, MM-dd-yyyy HH:mm:ss.fff, yyyy/MM/ddTHH:mm:ss.fff, yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fff, MM/dd/yyyyTHH:mm:ss.fff, MM-dd-yyyyTHH:mm:ss.fff, yyyy-MM-dd HH-mm-ss-tt.}

422.description: Unprocessable Entity

get.summary: New - Extensive search of device details.
get.description: This API returns device details, summarized product compliance details, smart groups, and custom attributes for enrolled devices. For unenrolled devices, only the device details are returned in the response.

parameters.name: organizationgroupid
parameters.description: OrganizationGroup to be searched. Admin's OG is considered if this is not sent.

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: Device platform.

parameters.name: startdatetime
parameters.description: Filters devices such that devices with last seen after this date will be returned.

parameters.name: enddatetime
parameters.description: Filters devices such that devices with last seen till this date will be returned.

parameters.name: deviceid
parameters.description: Device Identifier.

parameters.name: customattributes
parameters.description: Custom attribute names.

parameters.name: enrollmentstatus
parameters.description: Filters devices based on their EnrollmentStatus [Enrolled, EnterpriseWipePending, DeviceWipePending, Unenrolled].

parameters.name: statuschangestarttime
parameters.description: Filters the devices for which EnrollmentStatus has changes from enrollmentstatuschangefrom datetime. This filter is only for Enrolled and Unenrolled enrollment status.

parameters.name: statuschangeendtime
parameters.description: Filters the devices for which EnrollmentStatus has changes till enrollmentstatuschangeto datetime. This filter is only for Enrolled and Unenrolled enrollment status.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Specific page number to get. 0 based index.

parameters.name: pagesize

get.summary: New - Find relevant devices using various criteria.
get.description:
Returns details of relevant devices belonging to an enrollment user matching specified criteria, where results are ranked/sorted using the specified orderby criteria with maximum pagesize limit of 500.
If page size is greater than the maximum limit, it will return the first 500 records.
seensince and lastseen fields accept the following Valid DateTime formats :
yyyy/MM/dd, yyyy-MM-dd, MM/dd/yyyy, MM-dd-yyyy, yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.fff,
yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.fff, MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss.fff, MM-dd-yyyy HH:mm:ss.fff, yyyy/MM/ddTHH:mm:ss.fff,
yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fff, MM/dd/yyyyTHH:mm:ss.fff, MM-dd-yyyyTHH:mm:ss.fff, yyyy-MM-dd HH-mm-ss-tt,
yyyy-MM-ddTHH-mm-ss-tt.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: Filters devices based on enrolled username.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: Filters devices based on model. For example iPhone.

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: Filters devices based on platform. For example Apple.

parameters.name: lastseen
parameters.description: Filters devices based on the date when they were last seen.

parameters.name: ownership
parameters.description: Filters devices based on ownership type. One of C, E, S or Undefined.

parameters.name: lgid
parameters.description: Limits the search to given OrganizationGroup. Defaults to admin's OrganizationGroup.

parameters.name: compliantstatus
parameters.description: Filters devices based on specified compliant status. Possible values are true (for Compliant) and false (for NonCompliant).

parameters.name: seensince
parameters.description: Filters devices based on the date when they were seen after given date.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Filters search result to return results based on page number. Page numbering is 0 based and omitting this parameter will return the first page.

parameters.name: pagesize
parameters.description: Limits the number of search results per page. Defaults to 500.

parameters.name: orderby
parameters.description: Sort results based on given field. One of model, lastseen, ownership, platform, deviceid etc. Defaults to deviceid.

parameters.name: sortorder
parameters.description: Sort order of results. One of ASC or DESC. Defaults to ASC.

400.description: Bad Request - Organization Group with id :{} not found or Admin user does not have access to it

422.description: Unprocessable Entity

get.summary: Gets the list of Certificate Templates for a Certificate Authority (CA).
get.description:
Retrieves the list of Certificate templates for an organization group.
A Certificate template is a preconfigured list of certificate settings that allows users and computers to enroll for certificates without having to create complex certificate requests.
Enterprise certification authorities (CAs) use certificate templates to define the format and content of certificates, to specify which users and computers can enroll for which types of certificates,
and to define the enrollment process, such as autoenrollment, enrollment only with authorized signatures, and manual enrollment.
Associated with each certificate template is a discretionary access control list (DACL) that defines which security principals have permissions to read and configure the template,
as well as to enroll or autoenroll for certificates based on the template. The certificate templates and their permissions are defined in Active Directory® Domain Services (AD DS) and are valid within the forest.
If more than one enterprise CA is running in the Active Directory forest, permission changes will affect all enterprise CAs.

parameters.name: ogid
parameters.description: Organization Group Id.

parameters.name: certificateAuthorityId
parameters.description: Certificate Authority Id.

yyyy/MM/dd, yyyy-MM-dd, MM/dd/yyyy, MM-dd-yyyy, yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.fff, 
yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.fff, MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss.fff, MM-dd-yyyy HH:mm:ss.fff,
yyyy/MM/ddTHH:mm:ss.fff, yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fff, MM/dd/yyyyTHH:mm:ss.fff, 
MM-dd-yyyyTHH:mm:ss.fff, yyyy-MM-dd HH-mm-ss-tt, yyyy-MM-ddTHH-mm-ss-tt.

parameters.description: Unique identifier for the organization group

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: Flag to specify to get metadata or complete definition of a script

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter records based on the script name. Partial names are accepted.

parameters.name: script_type
parameters.description: Filter records based on the script type. Accepted values are [POWERSHELL] when platform is [WIN_RT], [BASH] and [PYTHON] when platform is [APPLE_OSX].

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: Filter records based on the platform. Default is None. Accepted values are [WIN_RT] and [APPLE_OSX].

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Filter records based on the script category.Default is None. Accept values like [IT-UTILITIES] and etc.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Specific page number to get. 0 based index. Default is 0

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Maximum records per page. Default 500

parameters.name: orderby
parameters.description: Name of the property used for sorting. Accepted values are [Name], [Platform], [ScriptType], [Category] and [ActionType]

parameters.name: sort_order
parameters.description: Whether the sort order is ascending or descending for specified orderby. The default property used for sorting is name. Accepted values are [Asc] and [Desc]. Default value is Asc.

403.description: Forbidden   1. {5145 : User does not have access to this organization group - {organization_group_uuid}.}

404.description: NotFound   1. {5177 : Organization group Uuid - {organizationGroupUuid} is not found.}

get.summary: Searches for Device Security Information for the device.

Processes the information like organizationgroup ID, user name, model, platform, last seen, ownership, compliant status,
            seen since parameters and fetches the security information for the same.

parameters.name: ownership

parameters.description: Specifies the date filter for device search, which retrieves the devices that are seen after this date e.g. [2017-02-03 01:33:07.383].

parameters.name: pagesize
parameters.description: Maximum records per page. Default 500.

get.summary: Searches for smart groups using the query information provided.
get.description:
Searches for smart groups using the query information (smartgroup name, organizationgroup Id, mdodifedfrom/modifiedtill date, page , pagesize) provided in the request query.
**modifiedfrom and modifiedtill** fields accept the following Valid DateTime formats : yyyy/MM/dd, yyyy-MM-dd, MM/dd/yyyy, MM-dd-yyyy, yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.fff, yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.fff, MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss.fff, MM-dd-yyyy HH:mm:ss.fff, yyyy/MM/ddTHH:mm:ss.fff, yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fff, MM/dd/yyyyTHH:mm:ss.fff, MM-dd-yyyyTHH:mm:ss.fff, yyyy-MM-dd HH-mm-ss-tt, yyyy-MM-ddTHH-mm-ss-tt.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: SmartGroup name.

parameters.name: organizationgroupid
parameters.description: Organization Group identifier.

parameters.name: managedbyorganizationgroupid
parameters.description: Smart group managing organization group identifier.

parameters.name: modifiedfrom
parameters.description: DateTime, Filters the result where SmartGroup modified date is greater or equal to modifiedfrom value.

parameters.name: modifiedtill
parameters.description: DateTime, Filters the result where SmartGroup modified date is less or equal to modifiedtill value.

parameters.name: orderby
parameters.description: Order by column name.

parameters.name: sortorder
parameters.description: Sorting order. Values ASC or DESC. Defaults to ASC.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number.

parameters.name: pagesize
parameters.description: Records per page.

400.description: 1.{6: ModifiedFrom should be less than ModifiedTill. But, {DateTime D1} is greater than {DateTime D2}} 2.{400: Resource's organization group not found or user does not have access to this organization group.}

get.summary: Retrieves the device details in the smart group.
get.description:
Retrieves the list of all devices with their respective details (such as DeviceId, Model, OS Version, Platform and Ownership) which belongs to a specific SmartGroup based on Smart Group Identifier.
**seensince and seentill** fields accept the following Valid DateTime formats : yyyy/MM/dd, yyyy-MM-dd, MM/dd/yyyy, MM-dd-yyyy, yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.fff, yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.fff, MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss.fff, MM-dd-yyyy HH:mm:ss.fff, yyyy/MM/ddTHH:mm:ss.fff, yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fff, MM/dd/yyyyTHH:mm:ss.fff, MM-dd-yyyyTHH:mm:ss.fff, yyyy-MM-dd HH-mm-ss-tt, yyyy-MM-ddTHH-mm-ss-tt.

parameters.name: smartgroupid
parameters.description: The SmartGroup Identifier.

parameters.name: seensince
parameters.description: Filters the devices in the smart group seen after the seensince datetime.

parameters.name: seentill
parameters.description: Filters the devices in the smart group seen before the seentill datetime.

400.description: 1.{6: ModifiedFrom should be less than ModifiedTill. But, (DateTime D1) is greater than (DateTime D2)} 2.{400: Smart Group not found with Id: or User does not have access to it.}

Searches for telecom device usage history based on the Organization Group ID, Alternate Id, Phone Number, Start Date, End Date.
*startdate and enddate* fields accept the following Valid DateTime formats : yyyy/MM/dd, yyyy-MM-dd, MM/dd/yyyy, MM-dd-yyyy, yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss.fff, yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.fff, MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss.fff, MM-dd-yyyy HH:mm:ss.fff, yyyy/MM/ddTHH:mm:ss.fff, yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fff, MM/dd/yyyyTHH:mm:ss.fff, MM-dd-yyyyTHH:mm:ss.fff, yyyy-MM-dd HH-mm-ss-tt, yyyy-MM-ddTHH-mm-ss-tt.

parameters.description: Organization Group ID.

parameters.name: searchBy
parameters.description: The Alternate ID type; possible values: [Macaddress, UdId, Serialnumber, ImeiNumber, EasId, DeviceId].(Formats: Macaddress: 848506B900BA, UdId: 6bf0f04c73681fbecfc3eb4f13cbf05b, SerialNumber: LGH871c18f631a, ImeiNumber: 354833052322837, EasId: 499NCV2JH55N5CUTR2D27GG42, DeviceId: 12345).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Device Alternate ID.

parameters.name: phonenumber
parameters.description: Search by device phone number.

parameters.name: startdate
parameters.description: Search by start date.

parameters.name: enddate
parameters.description: Search by end date.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Specific page number to get.

parameters.name: pagesize
parameters.description: Maximum records per page.

parameters.name: orderby
parameters.description: Orderby column name.

parameters.name: sortorder
parameters.description: Sorting order. Values ASC or DESC.

400.description: 1.{5005 : Invalid searchby parameter.} 2.{5005 : Invalid Phone Number format.} 3.{5005 : RangeFrom should be less than RangeTill.} 4.{5031 : Multiple Devices found for specified.} 5.{6 : Organization Group not found or Admin user does not have access to it.

404.description: 1.{5002 : Device not found for specified.}

parameters.description: Filter records based on the device name.

parameters.description: Filter records based on the device user. Accepted values are user's first name or last name.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter records based on update status. Accepted values are DOWNLOADING, DOWNLOAD_FAILED, INSTALLING, INSTALL_FAILED, DOWNLOAD_COMPLETE, INSTALL_COMPLETE, NOT_STARTED, IDLE

parameters.description: Filter records based on the reason for update status. Accepted values are IDLE, DOWNLOADING, DOWNLOAD_FAILED, DOWNLOAD_REQUIRES_COMPUTER, DOWNLOAD_INSUFFICIENT_SPACE, DOWNLOAD_INSUFFICIENT_POWER, DOWNLOAD_INSUFFICIENT_NETWORK, INSTALLING, INSTALL_INSUFFICIENT_SPACE, INSTALL_INSUFFICIENT_POWER, INSTALL_PHONE_CALL_INPROGRESS, INSTALL_FAILED, DOWNLOAD_COMPLETE, INSTALL_COMPLETE, NOT_STARTED

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number which will be fetched. 1 based index. Default 1.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to be returned in one page. Default 50.

404.description: 1. {5045: Device update with update uuid:{0} not found.} 2. {5177: Organization group with uuid {0} not found.}

\r\n INFORMATION – Logs general operational events that track the normal flow of the application.
\r\n ERROR – Logs only error events that indicate a failure in the current operation or flow.

organization_group_uuid.description: organization group (and below) to search relay servers

relay_server_type.description: Indicates the relay server type.| Specify 'UNKNOWN' to search relay servers of all types | Specify 'PUSH' to search relay servers of type PUSH| Specify 'PULL' to search relay servers of type PULL| Specify 'CLOUD_RELAY' to search relay servers of type CLOUD_RELAY

active.description: The state of relay server to search | Specify 'true' to search relay servers in active state | Specify 'false' to search relay servers in inactive state | If this attribute is not specified, the result will include both active and inactive relay servers

status.description: Indicates the status of the relay server used to track the upload status of the last interaction.| Specify 'UPLOAD_SUCCESSFUL' for last upload as successful. | Specify 'UPLOAD_IN_PROGRESS' for last upload as in progress | Specify 'LAST_UPLOAD_ERRORED' for last upload as errored.

search_text.description: Partial search text with compares to relay servers names.

limit.description: An integer that indicates the maximum number of results to return. Valid input range for this property is 1 to 500. (Default value - 50).

offset.description: An integer describing the starting position in the result set (Default value - 0).

sort_by.description: The column name on which the sorting is performed. eg NAME, TYPE, ACTIVE.

sort_order.description: The sort order of the results. eg ASC, DESC.

results.description: An array of relay server details containing the search results.

additional_info.description: Gets or sets Hypermedia content for the result.

PreviewDevicesSearchRequestModel.description: Request containing criteria for preview devices.

assignment_status.description:
Filter devices based on device assignment status. Supported assignment statuses are ADDED, REMOVED, UNCHANGED.
ADDED - List of devices for which targeted system update will be assigned and which didn't have previous targeted system update Assignment.
REMOVED - List of devices which had the given targeted system update assigned earlier, now will have targeted system update unassigned.
UNCHANGED - List of devices for which the given targeted system update is assigned as previous assignment.

last_check_in.description: Last Checkin time range with which results will be filtered.

search_text.description: Search text to search the assignment of given targeted system update for specific user or device.

offset.description: Offset to return. 0 based index. Default value is 0.

limit.description: Maximum number of records per page. Default value is 10.

sort_by.description: Column based on which we can provide the sorting. Supported values are LAST_CHECK_IN, ORGANIZATION_GROUP_NAME, PLATFORM, USER_NAME,DEVICE_FRIENDLY_NAME, ASSIGNMENT_STATUS. Default value is LAST_CHECK_IN.

sort_order.description: The order based which on we can sort. Supported values are ASC, DESC. Default value is ASC.

assignment_status.description:
Filter devices based on device assignment status. Supported assignment statuses are ADDED, REMOVED, UNCHANGED.
ADDED - List of devices for which targeted system update will be assigned and which didn't have previous targeted system update Assignment.
REMOVED - List of devices which had the given targeted system update assigned earlier, now will have targeted system update unassigned.
UNCHANGED - List of devices for which the given targeted system update is assigned as previous assignment.

newly_added.description: No. of devices for which targeted system update will be assigned and which didn't have previous targeted system update Assignment.

newly_removed.description: No. of devices which had the given targeted system update assigned earlier, now will have targeted system update unassigned.

unchanged.description: No. of devices for which the given targeted system update is assigned as previous assignment.

total_inactive.description: Count of devices that have not checked-in in the last 48 hours.
